violations.text: The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
Old rodent droppings were observed under the dishwasher and on the shelving in dry storage area. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH Center for Food Protection at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2025
The shelving in the back storage area was observed with an accumulation of old rodent droppings. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2025

The shelving and floors in the back storage room has an accumulation of old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/03/2025

Pork was observed thawing on the counter in stagnant water. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Food was moved to the food prep sink to thaw under running water. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024

At the time of inspection, cheeseburgers were observed held in the food service line hot hold unit at 90 to 123 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Burgers were placed into the oven for reheating at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2023
